blurb:
Pacific Buffet on Chastain Meadows is your go-to when you've got a group with wildly different appetites and you don't want to negotiate. All-you-can-eat sushi, tempura, and American fare under one roof, and the price point is about as low as you'll find in Marietta for what you get. Perfect for feeding a family on a budget or grabbing a quick lunch when you want options without committing to one thing.

The sushi is what most people come back for. It's not omakase, but it doesn't pretend to be. The tempura rounds things out if you want something hot and fried, and the American side of the buffet covers anyone in your group who isn't feeling adventurous that day.

Over 4,100 reviews at 4.1 stars tells you this place has been doing something right for a long time. It's not going to blow your mind, but it's reliable, it's affordable, and it keeps everyone at the table happy.
address: 2475 Chastain Meadows Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
Laredos on Sandy Plains is a solid neighborhood Mexican spot. Colorful inside, traditional decorations, the whole cantina feel. My wife and I end up here when we want Mexican without driving far, and it delivers on the basics every time. Good for a weeknight dinner, a casual date night, or just killing a Friday evening with chips and a margarita before you figure out the rest of the night.

The margaritas are worth ordering. They come up in reviews consistently and they're the kind of thing you end up getting a second one of before you meant to. The Mexican classics are what you'd expect from a place like this, done the way they're supposed to be done.

It's open late on Fridays and Saturdays, which makes it a natural landing spot after whatever else you've got going on.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
Marietta Melt Yard on Whitlock is the kind of spot you find on a Google search, decide to try, and then end up going back to. We took the family there and everyone had a solid time. Its a good call for a casual lunch with the kids, a group that can't agree on anything fancy, or just a weekday meal where you want something hearty without a lot of fuss.

The food is straightforward and filling. Reviews mention it can run on the greasier side depending on what you order, so go in knowing that and pick accordingly. If you're someone who wants lighter fare, maybe adjust your expectations. But if you want a real lunch that actually satisfies you, this hits.

Service has been consistently good from what we've seen and heard, which matters more than people give it credit for. The crowd is a mix of families and locals, the vibe is relaxed, and the napkins are apparently legendary. One reviewer thought they were cloth. High praise.
address: 800 Whitlock Ave NW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA

blurb:
This one is a legit hidden gem on Allgood Road and I mean that without any exaggeration. Its a supermarket with a taqueria inside, which sounds like nothing special until you eat there. The pastor is what people rave about most, and for good reason. Well seasoned, cooked right, not dried out. The kind of meat that makes you reconsider every other taqueria you've been going to.

If you want something different, the pupusas are outstanding. The bean and cheese version gets a 10 out of 10 from people who've tried it, and that's not nothing. The chicken plate with rice and beans is another solid move. The beans are made from scratch, not from a can, and you can taste the difference immediately.

This is the place you bring a friend to when you want to show off a local spot nobody else knows about yet. Don't sleep on it.
address: 786 Allgood Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
This place is a genuinely pleasant surprise. My wife found it and now we stop in more than I'd like to admit. Its a Brazilian bakery and cafe on Powers Ferry, open early and running late seven days a week. The vibe is bright and cheerful, exactly what you want when you need a coffee and something to eat before the day gets going. Good for a quick solo breakfast, a casual catch-up with a friend, or grabbing something sweet on the way home.

The traditional Brazilian cakes and cookies are what set it apart from any other cafe in the area. These aren't gas station pastries. They're made the way they're supposed to be made, and the light bites give you something more substantial if you need it. Everything comes out of a place that clearly takes the food seriously.

With 2,300-plus reviews sitting at 4.5 stars, it's not a secret anymore. But it still feels like one.
address: 1260 Powers Ferry Rd A, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
